note_strategy.name: slightly_awkward_compliment
note_strategy.description: Reference something specific about the person's LinkedIn profile — a recent post topic, an unusual headline observation, or a genuine niche interest. Must feel written by a human who actually looked at their profile. Avoids the AI-slop pattern of generic praise ('love your work', 'impressed by your journey'). One authentic observation + low-key reason to connect. No pitch in the note — relationship first.
note_type_priority: post_reference — reference a specific post from last 60 days (highest engagement rate — HANDS checks posts tab during search execution)
headline — reference something specific or unusual in their profile headline
interest — reference a genuine niche or hobby listed on their profile
generic — fallback if no posts, headline is bland, and no interests visible
hard_constraints: Under 200 characters (LinkedIn free account limit for connection note)
No mention of email campaign or any prior outreach
No pitch, no service offer in the connection note
No question marks — declarative or statement-based ending ('Good to connect.' not 'Would love to connect?')
No 'I came across your profile' — too generic
IMAP check per lead before sending connection request
Do NOT send if lead replied NOT_INTERESTED or UNSUBSCRIBE in email channel
note_strategy.character_count_note: 200 chars is ~30-35 words. Keep it to 3 clauses max. Count includes spaces and punctuation.

sector_templates._usage_note: Match template to lead by template_key field in batch_6_lead_notes. Fill all [bracketed] placeholders using actual profile data. Confirm final note is under 200 characters before sending. Priority: post_reference > headline > interest > generic.

trades.sector_label: Plumber / Electrician / Joiner / Builder / Roofer / General Contractor

post_reference.template: Hi [Name] — your post about [specific post topic, e.g. 'the bathroom refit in Knaresborough'] caught my eye. Not many tradespeople share the detail. Good to connect.
post_reference.usage: Use if HANDS finds a post from last 60 days in the profile posts tab
post_reference.fill_instructions: [Name] = owner first name from batch_6_leads.json. [specific post topic] = actual topic from post — be specific, not generic. Keep it to one clause.

headline.template: Hi [Name] — your headline made me look twice. [Specific observation about headline]. Good to connect.
headline.usage: Use if headline contains something specific, unusual, or unexpectedly honest — e.g. '20 years in the Dales', 'Dad, plumber, occasional rambler'
headline.fill_instructions: [Specific observation] = 1 clause referencing the actual headline text. Not 'interesting headline' — be specific.

generic.template: Hi [Name] — noticed you run [Business Name] in [Town]. Hard to find a good independent [sector] on LinkedIn. Good to connect.
generic.character_count_estimate: ~130 chars with typical fill values. Confirm under 200.
generic.usage: Fallback — use only if no post found and headline is generic
generic.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [Business Name] = exact business name. [Town] = town. [sector] = plumber / electrician / joiner / builder / roofer (match to actual sector).

post_reference.template: Hi [Name] — your post about [specific topic, e.g. 'the Harrogate market this spring'] was more honest than most agents manage. Good to connect.
post_reference.usage: Use if recent post found — market commentary, local insight, buyer/seller tip
post_reference.fill_instructions: [Name] = owner first name. [specific topic] = actual post subject. 'More honest than most agents manage' is the awkward compliment — adjust if tone feels off for the post.

headline.template: Hi [Name] — your headline is a bit different from the usual agency boilerplate. [Observation]. Good to connect.
headline.usage: Use if headline is unusually direct, personal, or sector-specific
headline.fill_instructions: [Observation] = 1 specific clause referencing their actual headline. Not 'interesting' — quote or paraphrase it.

generic.template: Hi [Name] — noticed you cover [Town] as an independent agent. Rare in this market. Good to connect.
generic.usage: Fallback — use only if no post found and headline is generic
generic.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [Town] = town. 'Independent' only if they are — remove if they're part of a chain.

hospitality.sector_label: B&B / Guest House / Pub / Tearoom / Café / Glamping

post_reference.template: Hi [Name] — your post about [specific topic, e.g. 'the lambing season guests'] was a proper read. Not many [town] hospitality owners use LinkedIn like this. Good to connect.
post_reference.usage: Use if recent post found — guest stories, seasonal updates, local events, behind-the-scenes
post_reference.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [specific topic] = actual post subject — be specific. [town] = town name.

headline.template: Hi [Name] — your headline is the most honest description of running a [B&B/pub/café] I've seen on here. Good to connect.
headline.usage: Use if headline is unusually candid or charming
headline.fill_instructions: [B&B/pub/café] = match to their actual business type.

generic.template: Hi [Name] — [Business Name] looks brilliant. Rare to see a [town] [sector] on LinkedIn. Good to connect.
generic.usage: Fallback if no post and headline is generic
generic.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [Business Name] = exact name. [town] = town. [sector] = B&B / pub / tearoom / guest house.

post_reference.template: Hi [Name] — your post about [specific topic, e.g. 'the probate backlog'] was genuinely useful. Not many local [solicitors/accountants] share that. Good to connect.
post_reference.usage: Use if recent post found — legal/financial commentary, local business advice, regulatory updates
post_reference.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [specific topic] = actual post topic. [solicitors/accountants] = match their sector.

headline.template: Hi [Name] — your headline is more direct than the usual firm boilerplate. [Observation]. Good to connect.
headline.usage: Use if headline is unusually specific or candid for a professional services firm
headline.fill_instructions: [Observation] = 1 clause referencing their actual headline text.

generic.template: Hi [Name] — noticed you practice in [Town]. Good to see local independents still holding ground. Good to connect.
generic.usage: Fallback if no post and headline is generic
generic.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [Town] = town. 'Independents' only if they are not part of a national chain.

headline.template: Hi [Name] — your headline caught my eye. [Specific observation]. Salons on LinkedIn are rare. Good to connect.
headline.usage: Use if headline is specific or charming

generic.usage: Fallback if no post and headline is generic
generic.fill_instructions: [Name] = first name. [Business Name] = exact name. [Town] = town.

orchestrator_status.status: NOT_IMPLEMENTED — src/outlocal/linkedin/ directory does not exist
orchestrator_status.build_urgency: CRITICAL — 61-session manual delivery failure. orchestrator.py is the ONLY mechanism that guarantees same-session execution. Without it: Batch 6 repeats the failure, archive trigger fires Tue 2026-06-10 EOD, all Batch 6 leads archived LOST_NO_RESPONSE on LinkedIn. No recovery.
note_sending_steps: 1. Read lead entry from batch_6_lead_notes by lead_id
2. Get profile URL from data/linkedin_profiles.json (written during search step)
3. Match sector template from sector_templates using template_key
4. If recent post found (flagged during search): fill post_reference template with actual post topic
5. If no recent post: check headline — if notable, fill headline template
6. Fallback: fill generic template with business name + town + sector
7. Confirm final note under 200 characters — hard limit
8. IMAP check per lead — if NOT_INTERESTED or UNSUBSCRIBE found: abort, skip this lead
9. Drive LinkedIn: open profile URL → click Connect → add note → send
10. Write entry to linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY after send attempt (success or failure)
